senses.definition: a means of transportation used to carry people or goods from one place to another, typically on roads or tracks

examples.x: Cars, buses, and trucks are all types of vehicles.
Electric vehicles are becoming more popular.
You need a license to drive a vehicle.
That vehicle is parked in the wrong spot.
The accident involved three vehicles.

senses.definition: a motorized vehicle designed for personal transportation

examples.x: After years of saving, she finally purchased her dream automobile, a sleek and stylish sports car.
In the early 20th century, the mass production of automobiles revolutionized transportation, making it more accessible to the general public.
Henry Ford revolutionized manufacturing with the assembly line, making automobiles more affordable for the average consumer.
Traffic congestion is a common issue in urban areas due to the high number of automobiles on the road.
Safety features such as airbags and anti-lock brakes are standard in modern automobiles to protect occupants in case of accidents.

senses.definition: a road vehicle that has four wheels, an engine, and a small number of seats for people

examples.x: I drive my car to work every day.
My father's car needs an oil change.
She forgot to lock her car before going into the store.
They are selling their old car and buying a new one.
We are going on a road trip and renting a car.

senses.definition: any type of vehicle that is powered by an engine

examples.x: He drove his motor vehicle to work every day, using it for his daily commute.
They rented a motor vehicle for their vacation to explore the countryside.
The motor vehicle's engine failed, leaving them stranded on the side of the road.
The new regulations require all motor vehicles to pass an emissions test.
The city has implemented new policies to reduce the number of motor vehicles in the downtown area.

senses.definition: a particular type or version of a product, such as a car model
a vehicle with two wheels, typically a bicycle or motorcycle

examples.x: She rode her two-wheeler to the park every morning.
He decided to buy a two-wheeler for his daily commute.
Children often learn to balance on a two-wheeler before moving to more complex bikes.
The city is promoting the use of two-wheelers to reduce traffic congestion.
After the repair, his two-wheeler was as good as new.

senses.definition: a vehicle with three wheels

examples.x: He drove his new three-wheeler around the neighborhood.
The company introduced an electric three-wheeler for urban transport.
She found the three-wheeler to be more stable than a two-wheeler.
The three-wheeler was perfect for carrying heavy loads in rural areas.
The delivery service switched to using a three-wheeler for better maneuverability in traffic.

senses.definition: a vehicle with four wheels, commonly referred to as a car, truck, or ATV

examples.x: I just bought a new four-wheeler for off-road adventures.
The mechanic said the four-wheeler needs a new transmission.
Four-wheelers are popular for their stability and versatility on various terrains.
She prefers riding her four-wheeler through the countryside during weekends.
The farmer used a rugged four-wheeler to navigate the muddy fields.

senses.definition: (of a vehicle) with the steering wheel on the left side

examples.x: The car he imported from Germany was left-hand drive.
In most European countries, vehicles are left-hand drive.
She found it challenging to adjust to a left-hand drive car after years of driving a right-hand drive.
The dealership specializes in left-hand drive vehicles.
They rented a left-hand drive van for their road trip across the United States.

senses.definition: (of a vehicle) with the steering wheel on the right side

examples.x: He found it challenging to adapt to driving a right-hand drive car in the United States.
The dealership specializes in importing right-hand drive vehicles from Japan.
She bought a right-hand drive sports car as a collector's item.
Driving a right-hand drive vehicle in a left-hand traffic country felt natural to him.
The right-hand drive configuration confused him at first, but he soon got used to it.

senses.definition: of something that does not require a physical key for operation

examples.x: The keyless ignition in modern cars lets you start the engine with a push of a button.
Our new apartment uses a keyless lock that operates with a code instead of a key.
The keyless remote for the garage door opener stopped working and needed replacement.
The keyless entry system allowed him to unlock the car with just a press of a button.
This hotel uses keyless technology for room access, which is convenient for guests.

senses.definition: a type of car transmission where the driver has to change gears by hand using a lever

examples.x: My dad taught me how to drive a stick shift when I was 16.
Learning to drive a stick shift can be challenging at first.
I rented a stick shift car for my trip to Europe.
His first car was an old stick shift truck.
She prefers driving a stick shift because it gives her more control over the car.

senses.definition: a vehicle operated or controlled by physical effort, typically involving a gearshift and clutch mechanism rather than automatic controls

examples.x: He preferred the feeling of control that came with driving manual.
Learning to drive a manual can be a valuable skill.
She had never driven a manual before and found it challenging.
Manuals are less common in the United States compared to automatics.
He stalled the manual a few times before getting the hang of it.

senses.definition: a vehicle that changes gears automatically without manual intervention
a vehicle that can drive itself without needing a person to control it

examples.x: The self-driving car safely navigated through city streets using its sensors and AI.
Many tech companies are investing heavily in developing self-driving cars.
She was amazed by the capabilities of the self-driving car during the test drive.
Self-driving cars promise to revolutionize transportation by reducing accidents and congestion.
Legislation regarding the use of self-driving cars is being debated in many countries.

senses.definition: a vehicle that can operate without a human driver

examples.x: The company is testing a new driverless car model in several cities.
Many people are excited about the idea of using a driverless car for their daily commute.
Some people are worried about the safety of driverless cars on the road.
The driverless car smoothly navigated the busy intersection without any issues.
I can't wait until driverless cars are available for everyone to use.

senses.definition: an automotive vehicle is a motorized machine designed for transportation on roads, typically powered by an internal combustion engine or electric motor

examples.x: The automotive vehicle accelerated smoothly onto the highway.
She studied automotive vehicles in her engineering class.
The dealership specializes in selling various types of automotive vehicles.
His dream job is to design innovative automotive vehicles.
We parked the automotive vehicle in the designated spot.

senses.definition: a type of transportation that moves on wheels, such as cars, trucks, bicycles, or wagons

examples.x: He preferred riding a bicycle over driving a wheeled vehicle.
The museum displayed ancient wheeled vehicles used by early civilizations.
The military uses various types of wheeled vehicles for different purposes.
She learned to repair wheeled vehicles as part of her mechanical training.
The wheeled vehicle navigated through the rough terrain effortlessly.

senses.definition: a type of vehicle that moves on tracks or caterpillar treads, commonly used in military vehicles, tanks, and some construction equipment

examples.x: The army deployed tracked vehicles to navigate the rugged terrain.
The tracked vehicle advanced slowly through the muddy field.
The military unit relied on tracked vehicles for rapid deployment in combat zones.
He admired the engineering of tracked vehicles for their ability to traverse difficult landscapes.
The construction company employed tracked vehicles to move heavy loads across the site.

senses.definition: a vehicle used for transporting goods or passengers for profit

examples.x: The company purchased a fleet of commercial vehicles for its delivery services.
Commercial vehicles are subject to different regulations than personal vehicles.
He drives a commercial vehicle for a living, transporting goods across the country.
The city implemented new rules to manage the parking of commercial vehicles downtown.
Commercial vehicles often have specialized equipment for their intended purposes.

senses.definition: a vehicle used by emergency services such as police, fire departments, or medical services to respond to urgent situations

examples.x: The fire department dispatched several emergency vehicles to the scene of the accident.
Police officers arrived in their emergency vehicles to handle the disturbance downtown.
She admired the sleek design of the new emergency vehicle used by the paramedics.
During the storm, emergency vehicles were on standby to assist with any emergencies that arose.
The city council allocated funds to purchase new emergency vehicles for the fire department.

senses.definition: a type of vehicle designed to be used on rough or uneven surfaces, like dirt trails or rocky terrain, rather than on smooth roads

examples.x: The off-road vehicle easily handled the rocky trail during our camping trip.
We rented an off-road vehicle for the weekend to explore the desert dunes.
After the heavy rain, we needed an off-road vehicle to drive through the muddy fields.
Off-road vehicles are essential for farmers who need to navigate through rugged terrain.

senses.definition: a vehicle designed for practical use, often for transporting goods or performing work tasks
a motorized or towable vehicle equipped with living amenities, designed for temporary accommodation and travel enjoyment

examples.x: They traveled across the country in their spacious recreational vehicle, stopping at national parks along the way.
The RV campground offered full hookups for recreational vehicles, including water, electricity, and sewage connections.
Many retirees enjoy the freedom of life on the road in their recreational vehicles, exploring new destinations year-round.
Recreational vehicles come in various sizes and styles, from compact camper vans to luxurious motorhomes with multiple slide-outs.

senses.definition: a vehicle that meets the legal requirements for use on public roads

examples.x: The company specializes in converting off-road vehicles into street-legal vehicles.
Motorcycles must comply with specific safety standards to be considered street-legal vehicles.
The new electric scooter model is advertised as a street-legal vehicle for city commuters.
They modified the dune buggy to make it a street-legal vehicle.
Before purchasing the ATV, he checked if it was a street-legal vehicle in their state.

senses.definition: a type of transportation powered by one or more horses pulling a carriage, wagon, or other vehicle

examples.x: They traveled through the countryside in a traditional horse-drawn vehicle.
The wedding procession included a beautifully decorated horse-drawn vehicle.
He learned to drive a horse-drawn vehicle during his summer job on the farm.
Horse-drawn vehicles were replaced by automobiles as technology advanced.
The museum exhibit showcased various types of antique horse-drawn vehicles.

senses.definition: a type of transportation that moves on its own without needing to be pushed or pulled by an external force, like a car with an engine or an electric scooter

examples.x: The Tesla Model S is a popular self-propelled vehicle known for its electric efficiency.
The military developed advanced self-propelled vehicles for rapid deployment in combat zones.
She commutes to work every day in a self-propelled vehicle powered by a small gasoline engine.
The construction site utilized self-propelled vehicles to transport materials efficiently.
He admired the engineering of the new self-propelled vehicle prototype displayed at the expo.

senses.definition: a vehicle designed to travel on a single narrow track, typically referring to motorcycles and bicycles

examples.x: The mountain trail was challenging for the single-track vehicle.
He enjoyed the agility of his single-track vehicle on winding roads.
Single-track vehicles require balance and skill to maneuver safely.
They explored the countryside on their single-track vehicles.
The single-track vehicle navigated smoothly through the narrow alleyways of the old city.

senses.definition: a type of vehicle characterized by a reduced width compared to standard vehicles
a vehicle capable of moving in any direction

examples.x: The omni directional vehicle effortlessly navigated tight spaces in the warehouse.
Engineers are developing omni directional vehicles for enhanced mobility in urban settings.
The omni directional vehicle showcased its ability to move sideways and diagonally.
Researchers are exploring new applications for omni directional vehicles in robotics.
The military is testing an omni directional vehicle for reconnaissance missions.

senses.definition: a type of transportation that is capable of operating on both land and water

examples.x: The amphibious vehicle smoothly transitioned from land to water.
We explored the lake aboard an amphibious vehicle.
The amphibious vehicle's propellers allowed it to move swiftly in the water.
Tourists enjoyed a scenic tour on the amphibious vehicle.
Amphibious vehicles are designed to operate on both land and water.

senses.definition: a vehicle that runs on fuel sources other than traditional gasoline or diesel

examples.x: Many consumers are interested in purchasing alternative fuel vehicles to reduce their carbon footprint.
The government is incentivizing the adoption of alternative fuel vehicles through tax breaks.
Hybrid cars are a popular type of alternative fuel vehicle that combines gasoline engines with electric motors.
Electric vehicles are considered one of the most promising alternative fuel vehicles for the future.
The company's fleet of alternative fuel vehicles includes both hydrogen fuel cell and compressed natural gas options.

senses.definition: a vehicle that is designed to have a minimal impact on the environment, typically by using alternative fuels or energy sources such as electricity or hydrogen

examples.x: The government offers incentives for purchasing green vehicles to reduce carbon emissions.
Their family decided to buy a green vehicle to lower their environmental footprint.
Electric cars are popular examples of green vehicles.
The company invested in a fleet of green vehicles to support sustainable practices.
Hybrid vehicles are another type of green vehicle that combines gasoline engines with electric motors.

senses.definition: a car that uses both a regular gasoline engine and an electric motor to save fuel and produce fewer emissions

examples.x: John drives a hybrid vehicle that switches between gas and electric power to use less fuel.
Hybrid vehicles recharge their batteries when braking, which helps them save on gasoline.
A hybrid vehicle can run on electricity alone at low speeds, reducing emissions.
Many taxi services are switching to hybrid vehicles to lower their operating costs and be more environmentally friendly.
Hybrid vehicles are becoming popular because they offer better gas mileage than traditional cars.

senses.definition: (of a vehicle) not producing gases harmful to the environment

examples.x: Electric vehicles are considered zero-emission because they do not produce tailpipe emissions.
The city aims to transition to a zero-emission public transportation system by 2030.
The government introduced tax incentives to encourage businesses to adopt zero-emission technologies.
Investing in zero-emission technology is crucial for reducing carbon footprints and combating climate change.

senses.definition: a car or truck that uses a lot of fuel, leading to higher fuel costs and more environmental impact

examples.x: His old truck was a real gas guzzler, costing him a fortune in fuel every month.
The environmentalist criticized the government's tax breaks for gas guzzlers.
They decided to trade in their gas guzzler for a more fuel-efficient hybrid.
The new regulations aimed to reduce the number of gas guzzlers on the road.
The company offered incentives to employees who switched from gas guzzlers to electric vehicles.

senses.definition: a car that has electricity as its power source instead of gasoline or diesel

examples.x: The electric car is gaining popularity due to its eco-friendly features and low operating costs.
She decided to buy an electric car to reduce her carbon footprint and save on gas expenses.
Many cities are installing charging stations to support the growing number of electric cars on the road.
The electric car can travel over 300 miles on a single charge, making it ideal for long trips.
With advancements in battery technology, electric cars are becoming faster and more efficient than ever before.

senses.definition: a vehicle powered by solar energy through photovoltaic cells integrated into its design, typically used for sustainable transportation

examples.x: The university's engineering team designed and built a solar car to compete in an international solar vehicle race.
In some regions, commuters use solar cars as a cost-effective and environmentally friendly alternative to traditional vehicles.
The futuristic concept car showcased at the expo was a sleek, aerodynamic solar car capable of long-distance travel solely on solar power.
Solar cars are gaining popularity in research and development labs worldwide as scientists strive to improve efficiency and reduce dependence on fossil fuels.
Governments are incentivizing the adoption of solar cars by offering tax breaks and subsidies to encourage environmentally conscious transportation choices.

senses.definition: a car designed and built for high speed, acceleration, and overall driving dynamics

examples.x: He dreams of owning a performance car that can accelerate from 0 to 60 mph in under 4 seconds.
The new model boasts advanced aerodynamics, making it a true performance car.
She took her performance car to the racetrack to test its handling capabilities.
Many enthusiasts prefer performance cars for their superior engine power and sporty design.
The dealership specializes in luxury performance cars from renowned manufacturers.

senses.definition: a car that has been modified or built according to the owner's preferences or specifications

examples.x: He spent years restoring and customizing his dream custom car.
The custom car show featured a wide variety of unique vehicles.
She decided to personalize her custom car with a distinctive paint job.
The custom car had a powerful engine and unique interior features.
He proudly displayed his custom car at the automotive expo.

senses.definition: a car that is owned and provided by a company to its employees to use for work-related purposes

examples.x: She was given a company car because she frequently visits clients in different cities.
The company car broke down on the highway, so he had to call for roadside assistance.
Employees who drive a company car must follow the company’s safety policies.
He accidentally scratched the company car while parking, so he reported it to his manager.

senses.definition: a small, economical car, typically with a focus on fuel efficiency and affordability

examples.x: He bought an econobox for his daily commute to save on gas.
She prefers driving an econobox because it's easy to park in the city.
The econobox has basic features but gets excellent mileage.

senses.definition: (of a car) having a design or characteristics that suggest speed or performance

examples.x: The sporty car zipped through the curves with ease.
She preferred a sporty vehicle that could accelerate quickly.
The sporty hatchback was known for its agility and responsive handling.
The sporty car zipped through the winding mountain roads effortlessly.
The sporty car had sleek lines and a low profile.

senses.definition: made up of distinct, connected parts that can move or bend

examples.x: The articulated trailer allowed the truck to make sharper turns.
The articulated joints in the toy allowed it to move realistically.
Her speech was perfectly articulated, capturing everyone's attention.

senses.definition: the ability of a vehicle to maintain traction and stability on the road surface

examples.x: The sports car's excellent roadholding allowed it to take sharp turns at high speeds.
A car with good roadholding performs well in both wet and dry conditions.
The SUV's all-wheel-drive system enhances its roadholding on rough terrain.
Manufacturers often test vehicles extensively to ensure optimal roadholding capabilities.
Drivers appreciate vehicles that offer reliable roadholding during sudden braking.
